test: libp2p re-validation of the session-3 perf path (does the async-send + pipelined ring carry to the REAL transport, not raw-TCP+PSK?)
transport: go-libp2p sidecar (TCP-over-libp2p tunnel, per-node ed25519 keys, Noise-encrypted, NO SHARD_PSK)
engine_swap: SHARD_TRANSPORT=libp2p in node_kv.py + specpipe.py swaps wire.py (ChaCha+PSK) for shard/transport.py; the engine is otherwise unchanged. New launcher: phase0/launch_libp2p.py (sidecar per node, -inbound + -forward tunnels; head sidecar also tunnels the coordinator's direct-return to the tail).

what_is_PROVEN_this_session.cross_box_libp2p_reachability: a 2 MiB activation-sized frame round-tripped NV->CA over libp2p in 192ms (sidecar self-test, authenticated by key, no PSK) — the transport works on these vast boxes
what_is_PROVEN_this_session.engine_runs_over_libp2p: with SHARD_TRANSPORT=libp2p the gpt-oss-120B stage engines warmed to 'listening' over the sidecar tunnels (transport swap + tunnel wiring correct); the perf change (async _AsyncSender) sits ABOVE send_msg, so it is transport-agnostic by construction

prior_proof_still_standing: docs/receipts/gpt-oss-120b-libp2p-20260619.json — the FULL 120B ring over libp2p, direct-return + pipelining, 44.79 tok/s warm, output BIT-IDENTICAL to the trusted-wire receipt (sha f646e0db…). So the perf path over libp2p is already proven at parity; async-send is an additive, above-the-wire change.
what_was_BLOCKED_and_why: A fresh end-to-end generation with THIS session's engine over a freshly-launched libp2p ring did NOT complete — blocked by vast SSH/daemon-launch flakiness, NOT by the engine or transport. Two failure modes hit repeatedly: (1) launching the detached sidecar daemon over ssh is intermittently unreliable (the daemon doesn't always survive the launching ssh's channel teardown), so a stage's forward connect to its local sidecar (127.0.0.1:29611) gets ConnectionRefused; (2) ssh rc=255 'try again in a few seconds' auth flakiness on these boxes dropped some launch calls. Both are fleet-ops issues orthogonal to correctness. A robust fix is an on-box launcher script (ssh triggers it, it backgrounds the daemon + returns) or `ssh -f`, rather than backgrounding the daemon inside the launching ssh.
conclusion: The libp2p TRANSPORT and the engine SWAP are validated (round-trip + engines warm over libp2p + the June-19 bit-identical full-ring receipt). The async-send perf win is transport-agnostic (it decouples compute from send_msg, whatever send_msg is). A clean re-run of the async-send A/B over libp2p remains the documented gap, blocked this session only by a flaky multi-daemon SSH bring-up — to be closed with a more robust launch harness.
